description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a rectangular stone base and a pitched roof supported by wooden beams. The stone base is constructed from roughly hewn, light gray blocks, forming a sturdy enclosure around the well opening. The roof is made of weathered wooden planks, some of which appear damaged or missing, and is supported by a red-painted wooden frame. The well sits on a patch of green grass, suggesting it is located outdoors, possibly in a park or garden. There are no visible mechanisms for drawing water, such as a pulley or bucket, and the well opening is uncovered. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements.
description: A rustic stone water well with a weathered wooden roof and red wooden supports, set on a grassy patch. The well lacks a cover or water-drawing mechanism and has a simple, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the stone base, red wooden supports, and the damaged wooden roof with missing shingles.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the stonework and the overhanging wooden roof, with more visible roof damage.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the stone enclosure and the back of the wooden roof, with the red supports clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear-right view showing the stone base, the underside of the roof, and the grassy ground around the well.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof supported by red wooden beams; no cover over the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Stone wall enclosure approximately waist-high, forming a protective barrier around the well opening; wooden roof provides overhead protection.
